surface: ov::genai::LLMPipeline with a triggered StructuralTagsConfig grammar + speculative decoding (draft_model)

environment.openvino_genai_source_commit: 7dea045 (releases/2026/2) -- the exact commit the baseline wheel was built from
environment.openvino_source_dir: C:/Users/mrbla/oss/openvino (build-x86_64/RelWithDebInfo, local dev build, CPU plugin linked; GPU plugin not linked in this local checkout)
environment.note_on_devices: Baseline reproduced on GPU (the officially reported/affected device). Patched-build verification run on CPU because the official Python .pyd is ABI-locked to the exact release build (a .dll-only swap fails DLL load), and a from-source Python-bindings rebuild against the local OpenVINO checkout has no linked GPU plugin. The bug is a sampler state-machine ordering error (IsTerminated() checked after FillNextTokenBitmask() instead of before), which is device-independent, so CPU is a valid substitute for proving the fix removes the crash.

methodology.grammar: triggered StructuralTagsConfig, trigger="<tool_call>", JSON-schema body (tool-call shape)
methodology.decode: greedy (do_sample=false), max_new_tokens=512, num_assistant_tokens=3 when draft attached
methodology.prompt_set: 19 generic conversational prompts (docs/upstream/725_xgrammar_stop_token/repro_prompts_generic.json), none requiring a tool call
methodology.reproducer_cpp: oss/openvino.genai/samples/cpp/text_generation/xgrammar_stop_token_repro.cpp (mirrors the Python reproducer; standalone C++ sample, not upstreamed)

results.label: baseline (unpatched, stock release wheel), GPU, spec-decode ON
patched (fix applied, rebuilt from source), CPU, spec-decode ON
results.note: same sample size as the baseline row above, for a clean before/after comparison

results.label: patched (fix applied), CPU, spec-decode OFF (control)
results.note: control: confirms the fix does not change behavior when speculative decoding is not in play

not_measured: patched build performance on GPU (no local GPU-plugin build available; performance is not the concern here, correctness is)
throughput/latency deltas from the fix (expected to be zero -- the change is a branch reorder, not a hot-path algorithm change)

crash rate beyond n=95 per arm (a larger N would tighten the confidence interval on the ~1% baseline rate, but 0/95 patched vs 1/95 baseline at matched sample size is the comparison that matters for this fix)
